violations.finding: Chinese characters inside a <USER_VERBATIM> tag: "又", "还是", "没修好", "第 N 次". Per Standard 6, verbatim non-English user quotes must reside in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only. This file is commands/dev.md (a command prompt file), which is in scope for the English-only requirement.
violations.recommendation: Move the verbatim Chinese quote to docs/dev/ticket-*.md (or a dedicated reference doc under docs/). In commands/dev.md, replace with an English summary of the retry signals, e.g.: retry signals (e.g. 'again', 'still broken', 'Nth time')

violations.finding: Chinese text block inside a <USER_VERBATIM> section (lines 1399-1403): verbatim user instructions in Chinese preserved in the command file. Per Standard 6, verbatim non-English user-binding quotes must reside in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only, not in command/agent prompt files.
violations.recommendation: Extract the verbatim Chinese user quote to docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md (already in scope exclusion zone). Replace in commands/dev.md with an English prose description: 'Orchestrator-prompt-purity rule originated from user requirement documented in docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md: orchestrator must describe WHAT only (problem, constraints, AC), never HOW (tool names, shell syntax).'

standards_checked_clean: no-inline-code (hooks/prompt-workflow.py, commands/dev-command.md, agents/dev.md — no executable inline code outside example blocks)
no-hardcoded-values (hooks/prompt-workflow.py uses parameterized paths; all script references in .md files are templates/examples)
use-source-venv (all python3 references in .md files are inside venv-activated permission pattern strings; prompt-workflow.py subprocess call is Python-to-Python, not a shell venv bypass)
integer-step-numbering (decimal step examples in agents/dev.md lines 632-635 are inside a BAD/GOOD example block, not structural step numbering)
meaningful-naming (no forbidden name patterns in any of the four changed files)
merge-similar-scripts (no new scripts created in this diff; hook is a pre-existing file)
concise-documentation (files are functional specifications; no marketing language or repetitive examples beyond structural necessity)
parameterized-scripts (hooks/prompt-workflow.py uses parameterized path resolution; no hardcoded environment-specific paths)
dangling-references (script references in .md files verified against filesystem; no broken paths)
merge-conflicts (no conflict markers found in any changed file)
